name: arc_case_matrix_v4_full_coverage_sharpened
objective: Minimize post-roundtrip val_bpb under quick-gate runtime guardrails and 16MB artifact compliance on promotion.
notes: v4 incorporates all reviewer tightenings: sharper hypothesis_statement, causal_story, strengthened null_result_interpretation, explicit execution_priority.
High-confidence lanes (3,6,7,8) untouched. Composite lanes (1,2,5,9) now single-mechanism first.
Ready for immediate AutoResearchClaw + quick_harness.sh execution starting with Lane 3.

lanes.name: Recurrence + LoRA + lightweight routing/gating composite
lanes.hypothesis: Given a recurrent shared-block baseline, adding low-rank specialization (LoRA) + lightweight gating will improve quality per byte.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we start from recurrence-only and add LoRA specialization plus residual gating, post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve at fixed artifact budget while keeping runtime under 1.10x baseline.
why_this_might_work: Recurrence gives effective depth at almost zero extra storage.
LoRA recovers loop-specific specialization cheaply.
Per-head or residual gating adds dynamic routing with tiny overhead.
direction: Use recurrence for effective depth at low storage cost.
Use LoRA to recover loop-specific specialization.
Add per-head or residual gating with minimal overhead.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, recurrence alone is sufficient and extra gating/LoRA adds runtime without quality payoff.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first (20 steps, seed=1337), then candidate. Parse val_bpb from log. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10. For promotion: artifact_bytes <=16000000 AND post-roundtrip val_bpb reported.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Testing whether recurrent shared blocks plus cheap specialization and gating beat the baseline on post-roundtrip quality without blowing runtime.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: split into minimal ablations (recurrence only / +LoRA / +gating / full)
define exact runtime baseline for the 1.10x ceiling
implement gating in the cleanest low-overhead form first

external_search_queries: recurrent transformer weight sharing lora gating

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'NUM_SHARED_BLOCKS':'3','NUM_RECURRENCE_LOOPS':'3','LORA_RANK':'8','LORA_ALPHA':'16','RESIDUAL_GATE_ENABLED':'1'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Recurrence gives cheap depth; LoRA + gating recovers specialization without full parameter cost.

lanes.name: Recurrence + MQA/GQA efficiency
lanes.hypothesis: Reducing KV-head count under recurrence frees bytes/runtime that can be reinvested to improve val_bpb.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we reduce KV-head parameterization under recurrence, the freed budget lets us preserve or improve post-roundtrip val_bpb while staying under the quick-gate runtime ceiling.
why_this_might_work: MQA/GQA drastically reduces KV cache and parameter count.
Recurrence can reinvest the saved budget into more loops.
direction: Tune MQA/GQA-style head allocation under recurrent depth.
Track quality/runtime balance using quick-gate runtime ceiling.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, attention efficiency savings do not translate to recurrent depth or the model is already KV-bottlenecked.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb and runtime_ms. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10. For promotion: artifact_bytes <=16000000.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Testing whether attention efficiency savings can be reinvested into recurrent depth or artifact budget.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define exact baseline attention setup
confirm whether runtime is secondary or a hard gate
run a small head-count sweep before broader search

external_search_queries: multi-query attention small language model efficiency

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'NUM_HEADS':'8','NUM_KV_HEADS':'2','HEAD_DIM':'64','NUM_RECURRENCE_LOOPS':'3'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: MQA/GQA reduces KV overhead; recurrence can convert saved budget into better effective depth.

lanes.name: QAT + roundtrip-aware compression alignment
lanes.hypothesis: Training aligned to the quantized/compressed deployment path preserves post-roundtrip quality.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we enable staged QAT aligned to int8+zlib roundtrip, post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve versus float-only training under the same gate budget.
why_this_might_work: Fake-quant matches the exact export path.
Compression-aware regularization prevents quality drop on roundtrip.
direction: Use QAT schedules that match the final int8+zlib roundtrip path.
Use compression-aware regularization and packing-aligned knobs.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the current int8 roundtrip is already lossless enough or QAT destabilizes small-model training.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10. Promotion requires post-roundtrip eval on the actual .int8.ptz file.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Making training match deployment so the final compressed artifact performs better after roundtrip.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: make sure fake quant path matches actual export path closely
define staged schedule variants
isolate whether gains come from QAT or compression regularization

external_search_queries: quantization aware training language model int8

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'QAT_ENABLED':'1','QAT_START_FRAC':'0.5','QAT_GROUP_SIZE':'32','COMPRESSION_REG_LAMBDA':'1e-4'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Matching train-time noise/regularization to the export path reduces roundtrip mismatch.

lanes.name: Tokenizer lane (strict gate)
lanes.hypothesis: Tokenizer choices can improve val_bpb but require stricter accounting and reproducibility checks.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If tokenizer vocabulary/algorithm is improved with strict accounting, val_bpb can improve without invalidating comparability.
why_this_might_work: Better tokenization compresses the validation set more efficiently.
direction: Evaluate tokenizer variants only after baseline accounting is stable.
Require stricter promotion review than other lanes.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain or accounting fails, tokenizer changes cannot be fairly compared under the current val_bpb metric.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Run verify_token_accounting(). Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10. Promotion requires manual review of token accounting.

full_path_checks: token accounting and dataset consistency review complete

lanes.what_we_are_doing: Exploring tokenizer gains carefully without invalidating comparisons.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define exact comparability rules
pin dataset/token accounting procedure
keep this out of early automated exploration until baseline is stable

external_search_queries: sentencepiece vocab size compression language modeling

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'VOCAB_SIZE':'4096','TOKENIZER_KIND':'spm'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); verify_token_accounting(); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Better tokenization compresses the validation set more efficiently.

lanes.name: Artifact-centric objective optimization (MDL/compression lens)
lanes.hypothesis: Adding a quantization-aware regularization term (fake-quant proxy + entropy) improves post-roundtrip score.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If the training objective includes a roundtrip proxy loss (fake int8 quant + entropy penalty), post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve at fixed artifact limit.
why_this_might_work: MDL penalty directly optimizes for the metric that matters after compression.
Structural sparsity favors parameters that quantize well.
direction: Treat compressed artifact quality as first-class objective signal.
Favor losses that preserve low-complexity structure under quantization/compression.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the current int8+zlib path is already near-optimal or the penalty destabilizes training.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Trying to train models that are naturally easier to compress well, not just accurate before compression.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: narrow the first concrete mechanism (MDL / sparsity / proxy loss)
avoid making this lane too broad
define one small first experiment

external_search_queries: minimum description length neural network compression objective

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'MDL_PENALTY_LAMBDA':'1e-5','COMPRESSION_OBJECTIVE_WEIGHT':'0.1'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Directly penalizing roundtrip fragility should improve compressed-model behavior at equal size.

lanes.name: Byte-grouped serialization for artifact compression
lanes.hypothesis: Byte-grouped packing improves compressed artifact size at comparable quality.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we serialize quantized weights with byte-grouped packing, artifact bytes will decrease without hurting post-roundtrip val_bpb.
why_this_might_work: Better packing reduces zlib entropy.
No change to model/training - pure packaging win.
direction: Compare byte-grouped versus default serialization path.
Hold model/training fixed and isolate packaging impact.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no size reduction, zlib already saturates or grouping introduces alignment overhead.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Train baseline once, then serialize in both modes. Evaluate roundtrip on each and compare artifact_bytes + val_bpb.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Testing whether better packing alone can improve leaderboard-relevant artifact quality/size.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define serialization interface cleanly
compare zlib levels and group sizes systematically
ensure eval truly uses roundtripped artifact

external_search_queries: byte grouping zlib compression neural checkpoints

lanes.starter_pseudocode: train_baseline_once(); for mode in ['default', 'byte_group_quantized']: artifact = serialize_checkpoint(mode=mode, group_bytes=64); evaluate_roundtrip(artifact); compare(val_bpb, artifact_bytes)
lanes.causal_story: Better packing reduces zlib entropy. No change to model/training - pure packaging win.

lanes.name: LAWA checkpoint averaging
lanes.hypothesis: Late-window checkpoint averaging improves final quality without increasing artifact bytes.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we apply lightweight checkpoint averaging near the end of training, post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve at negligible runtime cost.
why_this_might_work: Averaging smooths noise in the final phase.
Zero extra parameters - just post-process.
direction: Enable LAWA only in terminal phase.
Test interval/window sensitivity with fixed baseline settings.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the optimizer already converges to a flat minimum or averaging hurts the final export.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Trying a low-risk late-stage training trick to squeeze extra quality out of the same architecture.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define checkpoint save cadence
confirm averaging occurs before final export path
test with one stable baseline first

external_search_queries: checkpoint averaging low compute fine tuning

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'LAWA_ENABLED':'1','LAWA_INTERVAL':'10','LAWA_WINDOW':'5'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Averaging smooths noise in the final phase. Zero extra parameters - just post-process.

lanes.name: MTP training-only auxiliary heads
lanes.hypothesis: Training-only multi-token prediction heads can improve convergence with no artifact-byte increase.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we enable low-weight MTP auxiliary loss during training, final post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve with near-zero deploy-time cost.
why_this_might_work: Extra learning signal speeds convergence.
Aux heads discarded at export - zero byte cost.
direction: Enable MTP heads only during training.
Tune auxiliary loss weight to avoid destabilization.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain or destabilization, the auxiliary signal is not helpful for this tiny model scale.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Adding extra learning signal during training, then removing it for deployment.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define when auxiliary heads turn on/off
confirm no deploy artifact contamination
run a small loss-weight sweep
lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'MTP_NUM_HEADS':'2','MTP_LOSS_WEIGHT':'0.15'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Extra learning signal speeds convergence. Aux heads discarded at export - zero byte cost.

lanes.name: Value projections from shared representation (x0)
lanes.hypothesis: Deriving values from a shared latent representation (x0) reduces stored parameters while preserving quality.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If value projections are generated from a shared representation instead of separate tables, the model will achieve comparable or better post-roundtrip val_bpb with fewer stored parameters.
why_this_might_work: Reuses existing x0 representation.
Low-rank projection adds almost no parameters.
direction: Compare shared-value projection against baseline value-table path.
Track parameter overhead and stability.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, value tables are already optimal or the shared path hurts attention stability.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Testing whether value computation can share more structure and save parameters.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define exact baseline path being replaced
clarify expected savings mechanism
add one stability-focused ablation
lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'VALUE_FROM_X0_ENABLED':'1','VALUE_PROJECTION_RANK':'16'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Shared representation reuse cuts duplicated value parameters while preserving expressivity.

lanes.name: U-Net / mirrored skip structure
lanes.hypothesis: Mirrored skip structure can improve optimization efficiency under tight budgets.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we add a lightweight mirrored skip structure, convergence and final post-roundtrip val_bpb will improve for the same parameter budget.
why_this_might_work: Skip connections help gradient flow in deep-ish models.
Mirrored design reuses existing parameters.
direction: Add minimal mirrored skip links.
Constrain parameter increase and monitor runtime impact.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the model is too shallow for skips to help or the projection adds too much overhead.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Testing whether skip-style information flow helps small-budget optimization.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: define the smallest possible skip implementation
keep parameter growth tightly bounded
treat this as more speculative than lanes 3/6/7/8

external_search_queries: u-net skip connections transformer language model

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'MIRRORED_SKIP_ENABLED':'1','SKIP_PROJECTION_RANK':'8'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Skip connections help gradient flow in deep-ish models. Mirrored design reuses existing parameters.

lanes.name: Position/attention treatment tweaks
lanes.hypothesis: Targeted positional/attention tweaks can improve quality without large model-size changes.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we tune positional handling and attention windows, post-roundtrip val_bpb can improve without increasing artifact size materially.
why_this_might_work: Truncated RoPE or selective windows reduce noise.
Partial key offset improves positional encoding efficiency.
direction: Test partial key offset and truncated RoPE variants.
Test selective/local window settings under quick gate.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the default RoPE + full attention is already optimal for 1024 context.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate. Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Trying small attention/positioning changes that may yield quality gains cheaply.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: split this into single-change ablations
avoid testing too many knobs in one run
identify which positional tweak is highest priority first
lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'ROPE_TRUNCATION_FRAC':'0.75','SELECTIVE_WINDOW_SIZE':'256'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Truncated RoPE or selective windows reduce noise. Partial key offset improves positional encoding efficiency.

lanes.name: Advanced schedule/optimizer partition tuning
lanes.hypothesis: Late-stage optimizer and schedule partition tuning can unlock additional gains once architecture stabilizes.
lanes.hypothesis_statement: If we tune optimizer/schedule partitions after architecture selection, val_bpb can improve without violating runtime/artifact constraints.
why_this_might_work: Partition-specific LR and weight decay can fine-tune convergence.
Late-stage cosine restarts escape plateaus.
direction: Tune optimizer groups and schedule partitions after architecture lanes stabilize.
Prioritize low-risk adjustments with bounded runtime impact.
lanes.null_result_interpretation: If no gain, the default Muon+Adam split is already optimal for this model scale.
lanes.evaluation_plan: Run quick_harness.sh baseline first, then candidate (only after architecture lanes stabilize). Parse val_bpb. Require val_bpb < baseline AND runtime_ms <= baseline*1.10.
lanes.what_we_are_doing: Holding off a final tuning lane until stronger architecture candidates emerge.
what_still_needs_to_be_done: explicitly mark as downstream of architecture selection
define optimizer partition choices
avoid spending early search budget here

external_search_queries: learning rate schedule late phase transformer

lanes.starter_pseudocode: env = os.environ.copy(); env.update({'LR_SCHEDULE_VARIANT':'cosine_restart','WEIGHT_DECAY_PARTITION':'attn_vs_mlp'}); result = subprocess.run(['torchrun','--standalone','--nproc_per_node=1','/parameter-golf/train_gpt.py'], capture_output=True, text=True, env=env); m = re.search(r'val_bpb[:\s=]+([0-9.]+)', result.stdout); val_bpb = float(m.group(1)) if m else None; harness.report_metric('val_bpb', val_bpb)
lanes.causal_story: Partition-specific LR and weight decay can fine-tune convergence. Late-stage cosine restarts escape plateaus.
